paper.title: Nearby high-speed stars in Gaia DR2

extraction.summary: Large survey of ~1.5M Gaia DR2 stars with 5-sigma parallax, PM, and RV. Identifies ~100 high-speed stars; 25 with unbound probability pub >= 0.5. Only 2 have pub=1.00 (nearly 100% unbound) — Gaia DR2 5932173855446728064 and 1383279090527227264. Paper also identifies 19 PM-only candidates (no RV) with pub >= 0.95 from tangential motion analysis. Most candidates are likely statistical outliers of the bound population rather than truly unbound stars. Uses Kenyon+2018 Milky Way potential (more massive than MWPotential2014).

method_chain.summary: Gaia DR2 selection: 5-sigma parallax, proper motion, and radial velocity. Astrometric quality cuts: astrometric_gof_al < 20, visibility_periods_used > 9, astrometric_excess_noise_sig < 2. ~1.5M stars in 6-D sample. Additional 5-D sample (no RV) with same astrometric cuts for PM-only analysis.

Galactocentric distances and velocities computed correcting for solar motion. Kenyon+2018 Milky Way potential model (individual disk, bulge, halo components). R0=8.0 kpc. Galactocentric speed v_gc, radial component v_rrv, tangential v_tpm.

MC sampling of astrometric uncertainties → fraction of realizations on unbound orbits = pub (unbound probability). Pub >= 0.5 selects high-speed outliers. 3-sigma bound-outlier test distinguishes truly unbound from statistical flukes. For 5-D sample: uses tangential speed vtpm as proxy. Pub estimated from vtpm relative to local escape speed.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Gaia DR2 5932173855446728064: v_gc=747±3 km/s, pub=1.00. Identified in 6-D analysis of ~1.5M Gaia DR2 stars with 5-sigma parallax, PM, and RV. One of 25 high-speed outliers with pub >= 0.5. Marked as duplicated_source flag.Also in Marchetti+2018.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Probability of being unbound computed via MC sampling of astrometric uncertainties in Galactic potential. Pub interpreted as fraction of MC realizations on unbound orbits. Note: paper cautions high-speed stars may still be statistical outliers of bound population.

inclusion_assessment.summary: Gaia DR2 1540013339194597376: v_tpm=917.2±107.1 km/s, pub=1.00. Identified from tangential motion only (no RV available). One of 19 high-speed stars selected from 5-D Gaia DR2 data with 5-sigma parallax and proper motion. G=15.96, BP-RP=1.01.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: PM-only analysis: no radial velocity measurement available. Unbound probability estimated from tangential speed alone. Paper notes these are likely statistical outliers of the bound distribution; RV measurements needed for confirmation.
